fact_nuggets.text: Normalize (Japanese: Normal Skin) is an Ability introduced in Generation IV. It is the signature Ability of Skitty and Delcatty.

Description [ edit | edit source ] Games Description MD T D S Enables the Pokemon to use all of its moves as if they were Normal-type moves. B S L MD GtI Unavailable S MD All moves the Pokemon uses will be Normal type! MD RT DX All moves used by the Pokemon become Normal-type and deal more damage.

Contents 1 Effect 1.1 In battle 1.1.1 Generation IV 1.1.2 Generations V and VI 1.1.3 Generation VII onwards 1.2 Outside of battle Effect [ edit | edit source ] In battle [ edit | edit source ] Normalize makes all moves (including status moves ) used by the Pokemon with this Ability become Normal-type instead of their usual type . If Normalize makes Thunder Wave a Normal-type move, Thunder Wave can affect Ground-type Pokemon; Thunder Wave can only affect Ghost-type Pokemon that are either holding a Ring Target or affected by Foresight or Odor Sleuth . If the move Flying Press is affected by Normalize, it deals Normal / Flying -type damage. If the move Freeze-Dry is affected by Normalize, it will still do super effective damage against Water-type Pokemon. If a Pokemon with Normalize is under the effect of Electrify or Ion Deluge , all of its moves become Electric-type rather than Normal-type. Generation IV [ edit | edit source ] Hidden Power , Weather Ball , Natural Gift , and Judgment are affected by Normalize. Generations V and VI [ edit | edit source ] Hidden Power , Weather Ball , Natural Gift , and Judgment are no longer affected by Normalize. Similarly, Techno Blast is unaffected by Normalize. Generation VII onwards [ edit | edit source ] Normal-type moves used by the Pokemon with this Ability now receive a 20% power boost (precisely, a factor of 4915/4096), except moves that cannot be affected by Normalize. This boost applies to both moves that are originally Normal-type and moves that become Normal-type due to Normalize. Multi-Attack , Terrain Pulse , and damaging Z-Moves are unaffected by Normalize. Outside of battle [ edit | edit source ] Normalize has no effect outside of battle.

Makes the Pokemon's moves all act normal-type.
